data_in.description: Trained NeatMS neural network model file (default model or user-trained .h5 file)
Labelled validation dataset with peak annotations (High_quality, Low_quality, Noise classes)

data_out.description: True vs. False positive rate dataframe (Probability_threshold, True, False, False_low, False_noise columns)

domain_knowledge: The softmax activation function in the final neural network layer outputs three probabilities (one per class: High_quality, Low_quality, Noise) that sum to 1.0; threshold optimization operates on the High_quality probability specifically.

The optimal threshold balances true positive rate (sensitivity) against false positive rate (specificity): lower thresholds increase sensitivity but reduce specificity, while higher thresholds increase specificity but reduce sensitivity.
The default NeatMS model was optimized to threshold 0.22, which is the expected canonical value for the provided pre-trained model on typical untargeted LC-MS data.
The get_threshold() method selects the threshold that maximizes (True_positive_rate − False_positive_rate), which is an empirical criterion distinct from AUC or other statistical metrics.
The validation set must remain completely untouched during model training and is reserved exclusively for hyperparameter tuning to avoid data leakage.

direct_checks: verify file exists at github:bihealth__NeatMS containing the default NeatMS model
verify the get_threshold() method is callable on the loaded model object
verify get_threshold() accepts a labelled peak dataset as input
value of returned scalar from get_threshold() equals 0.22 (byte-for-byte exact match)
verify returned scalar matches result_default_model_threshold field value
expert_review: assess whether the criterion documented in result_optimal_threshold_criterion is statistically sound and appropriate for the LCMS signal classification task
evaluate whether the threshold value 0.22 is reasonable given the neural network model architecture and training data characteristics

[other] Trained NeatMS neural network model file (default model or user-trained .h5 file): 'Calling the method `get_threshold()` will compute and return the optimal threshold using the validation set that you can then pass everytime you use this neural network.'

[other] True vs. False positive rate dataframe (Probability_threshold, True, False, False_low, False_noise columns): 'Internaly, `get_threshold` call the method `get_true_vs_false_positive_df(label='High_quality')` which returns the following table'
[other] NeatMS: 'Calling the method `get_threshold()` will compute and return the optimal threshold'

cmd: from neatms import NeatMS; import pandas as pd; model = NeatMS.load('{inputs.trained_model}'); validation_data = pd.read_csv('{inputs.validation_dataset}'); threshold, metrics_df = model.get_threshold(validation_data, label='High_quality'); print(f'Optimal Threshold: {threshold}'); print(metrics_df); metrics_df.to_csv('threshold_metrics.csv', index=False)

expected_outputs: True vs. False positive rate dataframe (Probability_threshold, True, False, False_low, False_noise columns)
finding: NeatMS relies on neural network based classification to enable automated filtering of false positive MS1 peaks reported by commonly used LCMS data processing pipelines.
inputs: Trained NeatMS neural network model file (default model or user-trained .h5 file)
Labelled validation dataset with peak annotations (High_quality, Low_quality, Noise classes)
methodology_summary: Load trained NeatMS model and validation dataset containing labelled peaks across three classes (High_quality, Low_quality, Noise).
Invoke get_true_vs_false_positive_df(label='High_quality') to compute true and false positive rates across a range of probability thresholds (0.0–1.0).
Select the threshold value that maximizes (True positive rate − False positive rate) as the optimal decision boundary.
Return the scalar threshold and the underlying metrics dataframe for downstream use in peak classification.

Validation: Verify the returned threshold scalar is within expected range (0.22 ± tolerance for default model) and that the metrics dataframe contains complete probability-threshold rows with valid rate values between 0.0 and 1.0.
research_question: What is the optimal classification threshold value returned by the get_threshold() method when applied to a labelled peak dataset using the default NeatMS neural network model?

task_description: Invoke the get_threshold() method on a trained NeatMS neural network model against a validation dataset to compute the optimal probability threshold for peak classification, returning the scalar threshold value (expected ~0.22 for the default model).

task_inputs.label: Trained NeatMS neural network model file
Labelled validation dataset with peak annotations

task_objective: Execute the get_threshold() method on the default NeatMS model against a labelled peak dataset and verify the returned threshold scalar equals 0.22.

task_outputs.label: True vs. False positive rate dataframe across probability thresholds

title: Reproduce the Default Model Optimal Threshold Value via get_threshold()
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load a trained NeatMS neural network model and its associated validation dataset using NN_handler.create_model() and prepare labelled peak batches. 2. Call nn_handler.get_threshold() which internally invokes get_true_vs_false_positive_df(label='High_quality') to compute true vs. false positive rates across probability thresholds. 3. The method selects the threshold that maximizes (True positives minus False positives) and returns the optimal scalar value. 4. Verify the returned threshold matches the expected reference value (0.22 for the default model).
